requirements_summary.execution_model: real C++ host/device operation with on-device kernels
requirements_summary.inferred_math: layer normalization over the last logical dimension with same-shape output, epsilon, and optional affine weight/bias
requirements_summary.initial_layout_assumption: single-device tiled input to tiled output

references.reason: Closest host-side normalization wrapper in the target namespace; shows optional affine arguments, memory/kernel config forwarding, and separation between host-side prep and prim launch.
Shows normalization-module device-op validation, output tensor spec creation, and device_operation launch plumbing.
Closest accessible normalization program factory with CB setup, reader/compute/writer kernels, epsilon handling, and optional weight/bias tensor wiring.
Best accessible reference for last-dimension reduction scheduling and per-row work partitioning, which layernorm needs for normalization over the final axis.
Shows per-op nanobind binding style, docstring layout, and keyword argument exposure pattern for normalization ops.
Shows how a normalization op is registered into the module-level nanobind entrypoint.
Shows normalization library source registration, kernel globbing, and install layout that layernorm must extend.

assumptions: Initial implementation scope is single-device and non-distributed; distributed layer_norm_pre_all_gather and layer_norm_post_all_gather are out of scope for this discovery run.
Initial data path is on-device tiled input to tiled output; no row-major tilize/untilize references were selected.
Layernorm is assumed to normalize over the last logical dimension and preserve the full input shape.
Optional affine weight and bias are in scope because local tests and docs use them.
Optional residual_input_tensor support is not assumed to be part of the minimum target, but later phases should reconcile it against the existing local API/tests.
The current working tree does not contain checked-in normalization/layernorm or normalization/rmsnorm source files, so downstream phases must derive the implementation from the selected references.

candidate_operations_considered.reason: Closest accessible TTNN normalization op with the required host/device/integration surfaces.
Best accessible reference for last-dimension reduction mechanics.
Contains useful reduction-and-normalization ideas but is too specialized around gating/top-k behavior.
Mentioned by local tests, docs, build artifacts, and kernel-library notes, but the actual source files are missing from the current working tree.
Confirms intended prim layer_norm call shape, but depends on missing normalization/layernorm headers and is not a usable primary reference.

observations: ttnn/ttnn/operations/layernorm exists in the current working tree but only contains pycache and the references directory.
ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/normalization/layernorm exists as empty directories in the current working tree.
Local docs and tests already exercise ttnn.layer_norm, ttnn.rms_norm, and distributed norm API names.
Local kernel-library markdown documents mention layernorm and rmsnorm kernel families even though the source files are absent from the current working tree.
